For "0.9", count becomes 0. The loop `for(i=1;i<=1;i++)` swaps s[2] and s[1]. s was "0.9\0". s[1]='.', s[2]='9'. After swap: s[1]='9', s[2]='.'. String is "09.\0". The print loop starts at i=1. It prints '9', then '.', then stops at '\0'. Output "9.e-1". The expected output is "9e-1". The code fails to remove the decimal point when there are no fractional digits remaining after normalization.
